about.title: About Me
about.intro1: I’m a fullstack developer focused on building structured and maintainable web applications with clear separation between frontend and backend systems.
about.intro2: I enjoy working with authentication flows, REST APIs, database integration, and responsive UI architecture. I value clarity in both code and design.

bookmate.shortDescription: A fullstack web application for managing books and quotes with secure JWT-based authentication.
bookmate.longDescription: Bookmate is a fullstack web application built with Angular and ASP.NET Core Web API that allows users to organize books and save quotes in a personal collection. The application uses JWT-based authentication to securely manage user sessions and protect API routes. It demonstrates RESTful API integration, a structured client-server architecture, and full CRUD functionality for both books and quotes.

features.jwt: JWT-based authentication and protected API routes
features.crud: CRUD functionality for books and quotes

screenshots.booksDesktop: 'My Books' page allowing users to add, edit, and delete books.
screenshots.booksMobile: 'My Books' page on mobile, fully responsive with complete CRUD functionality.
screenshots.addQuote: Form for adding new quotes to the user's collection.
screenshots.themeToggle: Light and dark mode toggle with smooth theme transition.

freakyfashion.shortDescription: A fullstack e-commerce web application with product management, cart functionality, and full CRUD operations.
freakyfashion.longDescription: Freaky Fashion is a fullstack e-commerce application built with React (Vite + TypeScript) and an Express + SQLite backend. The platform includes both a customer storefront and a dedicated admin panel for structured content management. The project demonstrates full CRUD operations through a RESTful API, persistent relational data storage, and a scalable component architecture. It emphasizes separation of concerns between frontend and backend, structured routing, and maintainable state handling across complex user flows such as search, cart management, and checkout.

features.apiCrud: Full CRUD operations via REST API

screenshots.productDetails: Product detail view with dynamic content rendering.
screenshots.cart: Shopping cart with quantity updates and product removal functionality.
screenshots.checkout: Responsive checkout flow optimized for mobile devices.
screenshots.adminProducts: Admin product list with search filtering and delete functionality.
screenshots.adminCategory: Admin interface for creating categories and managing products via SKU search.
screenshots.adminDetails: Admin product detail view with publish and unpublish controls.

levelup.shortDescription: A database-driven web application with role-based access and server-side rendering.
levelup.longDescription: LevelUp is a fullstack web application built with Node.js, Express, EJS, and SQLite. The platform allows users to browse games, write and interact with reviews, and engage with content through a structured server-rendered architecture. The project demonstrates database integration, role-based user management, and dynamic page rendering using EJS templates. It integrates external game data via the RAWG API and combines persistent SQLite storage with modular backend routing and MVC-inspired structure.
levelup.role:
Implemented the responsive header and mobile navigation system, ensuring consistent behavior across desktop and mobile devices. Designed and developed the full review details page, structuring dynamic content rendering based on game data. Built a category-based carousel to display related games using conditional logic and external API integration.

Developed the client-side voting and comment system with cookie-based state persistence to maintain user interactions across sessions. Implemented conditional UI rendering based on authentication status and user roles (guest, user, admin), ensuring proper permission handling and a clear, intuitive user experience.

workflow.scrum: Scrum-based agile development with sprint planning and daily stand-ups

workflow.git: Version control using Git and GitHub
